titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/249 (1)

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=226 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:07:10.539386+00 | translation moa=227 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:07:08.558844+00

p.cl: Iohannes Dei gratia Candide case Episcopus Omnibus sancte matris Ecclesie filiis  fidelibus salutem.

Nouerint presentes  futuri . nos tocius capituli nostri assensu pro salute anime nostre Omniumque predecessorum nostrorum successorumque
Animarum . salute  concessisse  Episcopali auctoritate presentisque scripti testimonio confirmasse Deo  Ecclesie Sancte crucis de edenburg .  Canonicis ibidem Deo Seruientibus  seruituris in liberam  puram  perpetuam
Elemosinam . Omnes Ecclesias quas bone memorie Fergus  filius eius Huctredus . & DauiD filius Terri cum terris eisdem Canonicis diuine remuneracionis intuitu assensu diocesani Episcopi contulerunt   Cartis suis confir
mauerunt. Ex donacione Scilicet fergus  Ecclesiam de Dunroden cum tota uilla de Dunroden  Omnibus pertinenciis suis .  Insulam de Trahil .  Galtweith cum Ecclesia eiusdem uille  Omnibus pertinenciis
Suis . Ex donacione uero huctredi filii eius  Ecclesiam Sancti Cuthberti de Desnesmor .  Ecclesiam de Tuncgeland .  Ecclesiam de Twenham .  Ecclesiam sancti Andree .  Ecclesiam de lockelletun .  Ecclesiam
de kyrkecormac cum Capella de Balnecros . Et ex donacione DauiD filii Terri  Ecclesiam de Anewith cum Capella de Culenes   omnibus pertinenciis suis . Quare uolumus  Episcopali auctoritate concedimus  ut predicti ca
nonici Omnes prenominatas Ecclesias cum Capellis  Omnibus obuencionibus suis .  cum terris  Omnibus pertinenciis suis  prout sibi placuerit in usus proprios totaliter conuertant .  eas libere  quiete  solute ab omni procuracione  consuetudine
 exactione inperpetuum teneant  possideant  Easdemque Ecclesias per Capellanos sibi congruos nobis uel successoribus nostris presentatos . uel per semet ipsos prout sibi expedire uiderint  deseruiri faciant . minusque
idoneos consulto tamen Episcopo  amoueant . Saluis nobis  successoribus nostris tantum sinodalibus .  communibus auxiliis racionabilibus de predictis Ecclesiis . exceptis de kyrkecormac cum Capella de Balnescors .  lockelletun Ecclesiis 
que per predecessorem nostrum bone memorie christianum Episcopum  per nos a sinodalibus  ceteris episcopalibus penitus sunt exempte.

Hiis testibus . mich’ priore de Wytherne  eiusdem loci capitulo . Domino Alano filio Rolandi .
Constabulario Domini Regis Scottorum . Iohanne Archidiacono . Matheo decano de Desnes . Willelmo decano de farenes . Gilberto decano de Rennes . Waltero persona . Durando persona de Magof . Martino
clerico de kyrkecuthbert  Aliis quam pluribus.
John by the grace of God Bishop of Whithorn to all sons and faithful of Holy Mother Church, Greeting:

Let present and future come to know that with the agreement of the whole of our chapter in return for the salvation of our soul and the salvation of the souls of all our predecessors and successors, we have granted and by episcopal authority confirmed by the testimony of the present document, to God and to the church of Holyrood at Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, in free and pure and perpetual alms, all the churches with their lands that Fergus of good memory, and his son Uhtred, and David son of Terrus, inspired by divine reward, bestowed to the same canons by the agreement of the diocesan bishop, and made firm by their own charters. That is, from the donation of Fergus, the church of Dunrod with the whole toun of Dunrod and all its pertinents; and the isle of Trail; and Galtway with the church of that toun and all its pertinents. From the donation of Uhtred, the church of St Cuthbert of Desnesmor; and the church of Tongland; and the church of Twynholm; and the church of St Andrew; and the church of Kelton; and the church of Kirkcormack with the chapel of Barncrosh. And from the donation of David son of Terrus, the church of Anwoth with the chapel of Culenes and all its pertinents. Wherefore we will and grant by episcopal authority that the aforesaid canons may completely convert to their own benefit all the aforenamed churches with the chapels and all their revenues, and with lands and all their pertinents as they please, and hold and possess them freely and quietly and released from all procuration and custom and exaction in perpetuity, and they shall cause those churches to be served by chaplains agreeable to themselves, presented by us or our successors, or by themselves if it seems expedient to them, and may remove those less suitable, in consultation with the bishop. Reserving to ourself and our successors only reasonable synodals and common aids from the aforesaid churches, except the churches of Kirkcormack, with the chapel of Barncrosh, and Kelton, which were entirely exempted from synodals and the rest of the episcopal dues by our predecessor of good memory, Bishop Christian, and by us.

With these witnesses, Michael the prior of Whithorn, and the chapter of the same place, the lord Alan son of Roland, constable of the lord King of Scots, John the archdeacon, Matthew the dean of Desnes, William the dean of Farines, Gilbert the dean of Rhinns, Walter the parson, Durand the parson of Minnigaff, Martin the clerk of Kirkcudbright, and very many others.
